rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' et al VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn In Support Of Mtn For Attorneys' Fees Pursuant To 42 U.S.C. Sec 1988
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 28, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANTS STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINSTRATION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS, BRIGID HANSON, RODERICK HICKMAN AND TIM VIRGA'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees Pursuant To 42 U.S.C. Sec. 1988 is denied. The Court considers this to be a motion to reconsider its prior order rejecting the request for fees under CCP 1038, not a request to reconsider Judge Alvarado's order of June 29, 2006. The Court believes that the issue of the frivilous nature of this litigation warrants consideration but defendant may not do so on a piecemeal basis. The issue of the right to attorneys' fee for a 1983 action was the principle point addressed at oral argument. That was the time for defendant to advance this argument. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R TOZZI et al VS. WALTER E. MCGUIRE REAL ESTATE, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 28, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT HENRY SHINEFIELD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained based on the statute of limitations. The amended complaint alleges that as early as 2002, plaintiffs questioned the authenticity of the second offer but did nothing about their concerns. The amended complaint also fails to allege facts that establish a basis for a duty owed to plaintiffs or that the alleged wrongful act - backdating - caused plaintiffs any harm for plaintiffs already had agreed to purchase the subject property. Hearing required if plaintiffs seek leave to amend. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LES JANKEY et al VS. SONG KOO LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Atty Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 28, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT SONG LEE'S Motion For Attorney Fees. The motion is granted based on the decision in Molski v. Ariero Wine Group (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 786 that holds a claim need not be frivolous to support an attorneys' fee award to defendant where plaintiff unsuccessfully seeks a disability access injunction. This case alleges the identical causes of action as in Molski and that Court's analysis is controlling on defendant's right to attorneys fees. In this case, plaintiff Disability Rights Enforcement Education Services ("DREES") lacked standing, a holding previously applied to DREES in Molski v. Mandarin Torch Restraurant (2005) 359 F.Supp. 924. As the Court's summary judgment order made clear, Mr. Jankey's claims were all premised on a four foot step leading into a market in a 50 year old building. The parties agreed on the applicable law, Colorado Cross Disability Coalition v. Hermanson Family Limited (2001) 264 F.3d 999, 1002 that plaintiff had to establish that the removal of the barrier to entry is readily achievable. Yet, plaintiff's evidence was inadequate as a matter of law. Given the extensive experience of plaintiff's counsel in these cases, it may be reasonably inferred that he and, in turn his clients, knew just how problematic this litigation was. Plaintiffs' contend that they lacked knowledge that the building had not been remodeled until receiving defendant's interrogatory responses. Yet, they present no evidence to suggest that they had any reason to believe the building had been remodeled so as to require ADA improvements and there is no evidence plaintiffs sought records from the City of San Francisco as to any permits to remodel the premises. The Court finds that billing rates for defendant's counsel are reasonable; in fact, the rates are at the low end of the San Francisco market. The Court awards fees of $106,206 baed on defendant's categories as follows: - General handling/management: $19,7

rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 28, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled; 10 days to answer. As to the fraud claim, the amended complaint alleges the LANS and it lawyers acted as counsel for plaintiff. This allegation is sufficient to allege a fiduciary duty. Defendant's argument as to the immunity of the Regents does not dispose of the cause of action which alleges wrongful conduct on the part of LANS after the assignment in paragraphs 33 and 34. The equal access argument is a factual, not a pleading issue. The absence of a copy of the license agreement precludes the Court from sustaining the demurrer based on the contention that only a breach of contract is being alleged. The demurrer to the implied covenant also cannot be addressed on demurrer where the contract is not a part of the record. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JACK WILKEN VS. FRUITVALE HEALTHCARE CENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 28, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT SSC OAKLAND FRUITVALE OPERATING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The allegations fail to allege recklessness. See Mack v. Soung (2000) 80 Cal. App. 4th 966, 972-973. =(302/PJM/VC)
